hard.next_step: 19th May: Meeting 26th July 2026 onsite, alot of restructuring and they are currently trying to get a business case approved for a formal evaluation. Send follow-up email recap + Workday integration documentation (API/integration overview for IT). Schedule live demo (preferably onsite in Melbourne) focused on: Supplier Risk & Performance (CPS 230 / material service providers / 4th party), Contract renewals + compliance visibility (AI assist), Spend visibility by supplier (holistic view), Teams-based intake (reduce maverick buys). Share Horizon event invite (Apr 21-22) for Saud to consider/forward to Adam.
hard.ais_why: Netwealth Investments is a digital-first financial services organization with a strong focus on technology and operational efficiency. While there is no public evidence of a dedicated AI-led procurement transformation, the company has demonstrated continued investment in digital innovation and automation. Its procurement function is likely to benefit from AI-powered intake management, sourcing, contract lifecycle management (CLM), supplier risk management, and spend analytics to improve compliance, accelerate procurement cycles, and enhance decision-making. As AI adoption continues to grow across financial services, Netwealth is a strong candidate for evaluating Agentic AI-enabled Source-to-Pay (S2P) solutions.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal re-warmed: after a May pause, Adam Baker and Saud Abdul Rahaman agreed on 1 July to a high-level Melbourne onsite demo, now scheduled 24-26 July. The deal is genuinely live and engaged, but the 17 Nov 2026 close is premature: Saud stated on the 2 April call they will only start their formal evaluation in November-December 2026, after their 15-month Workday rollout (started April 2026, completes ~Q3 2027) stabilizes, and are not implementing in parallel. Realistic decision window is 2027, so the current date will slip. Still single-threaded through Procurement (Adam, Saud), no economic buyer mapped, and CFO/CEO cost skepticism to overcome.
north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 134 (today 2026-07-06 to close 2026-11-17). Qualified 2026-06-22 (14 days in Qualified as of 2026-07-06); no Formal_Eval or Shortlisted submission dates, so milestone time-in-stage cannot be computed. Close date pushed +96 days from 2026-08-13 to 2026-11-17 on 2026-07-02. Buyer-stated formal evaluation start: November-December 2026, after Workday stabilizes (15-month program), so a realistic close is Q1-Q2 2027, roughly 6+ months beyond the current date. Pace to hit 17 Nov would require demo, multi-thread to IT/EB, quantified ROI, business-case approval and shortlisting inside ~18 weeks, which the buyer's own stated sequence rules out.
evidence: Deal re-engaged: Adam Baker and Saud Abdul Rahaman agreed a high-level demo for July (Task 2026-07-01), with a follow-up meeting/onsite demo scheduled (Event 2026-07-24; Next_Step onsite 26 July 2026)
Buyer-stated timeline defers real evaluation: Saud said they plan to 'start working on it from November, December this year' by finalizing requirements like an RFP, then evaluate (Avoma 2026-04-02)

Not running in parallel with Workday: 'we are not even looking at implementing in parallel with Workday because we already have a lot of priorities at the moment' (Saud, Avoma 2026-04-02); Workday is a 15-month program that just started
Close date slipped forward +96 days from 2026-08-13 to 2026-11-17 on 2026-07-02 (OpportunityFieldHistory, by Luke Dougherty)
Economic buyer unmapped: business-case approval sits with top management / CFO-CEO who are not identified or engaged (email task 2026-05-21; Avoma 2026-04-02)

Cost-justification pressure: Adam relayed the CFO/CEO view 'you can do it in a spreadsheet... or spend $100,000 a year on a platform... I think we'll just keep using the spreadsheet' (Avoma 2026-04-02)

Single-threaded: only two contact roles, Adam Baker (Evaluator/Procurement Manager) and Saud Abdul Rahaman (Influencer/Procurement Specialist); no IT/Workday, finance, or C-level contact (OpportunityContactRole)
Pain is real but unquantified: ~498 suppliers managed manually via spreadsheets/emails under APRA CPS 230 ('it is consuming my life'), no cost, cycle-time or savings figure built (Avoma 2026-04-02)

metrics.narrative: Adam Baker (Procurement Manager) articulated the pain in detail but never quantified it: Netwealth built its procurement function from scratch (Adam started May 2024, Saud Aug/Sep 2024) and manages ~498 suppliers manually via spreadsheets, emails and questionnaires under APRA CPS 230, which Adam called 'consuming my life' (Avoma 2026-04-02). No numeric impact exists yet, no cycle-time, headcount, savings or audit-cost figure, and the CFO/CEO frame it as 'a spreadsheet takes an hour, a $100k platform takes 45 minutes, keep the spreadsheet' (Avoma 2026-04-02). Quantifying CPS 230 audit-readiness cost avoidance and manual-effort savings is the single most important move to beat that inertia.
sources: Avoma 2026-04-02 (Adam Baker on CPS 230, ~498 suppliers, manual spreadsheets)
Avoma 2026-04-02 (CFO/CEO 'spreadsheet vs $100k' view relayed by Adam)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named or engaged. Budget authority sits with top management (CFO/CEO): Adam recounted asking his manager for procurement investment and being told 'a finger was pointed at me and said, you're the investment' (Avoma 2026-04-02), i.e. headcount/tool spend was declined. Saud confirmed progression is gated on that approval: 'our priorities have been changed for the next fiscal year... I will reconnect with you once [top management approves the business case]' (email task 2026-05-21). The evidence shows the budget owner is the CFO/CEO but none is mapped by name, so identifying and reaching them via Adam is a ranked move.
sources: Avoma 2026-04-02 (Adam: 'you're the investment')
Email task 2026-05-21 (Saud: awaiting top-management business-case approval)
SF Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c false

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer stated clear, consistent criteria across the discovery call: a single holistic view of ~498 suppliers with compliance/risk status and contract-renewal visibility, spend visibility per supplier, Teams-based intake to stop maverick buying, and AI that assists rather than decides ('AI still requires human intervention... if that is speeding up the process a little bit, that's what we are expecting', Saud, Avoma 2026-04-02). Configurability and user-friendliness are explicit tests: 'at what degree we can configure it... and how user friendly is that' (Saud, Avoma 2026-04-02). Workday integration is a named must-have. These are confirmed as wants but not yet formalized into an RFP/BRD, which the buyer said they will write around November-December 2026.
sources: Avoma 2026-04-02 (Saud/Adam: holistic supplier view, spend visibility, Teams intake, configurability, AI-assist)
SF Next_Step__c (CPS 230 supplier risk, contract renewals, Teams intake, spend visibility)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer described the process as sequential and deferred: they are gathering information now ('expression of interest and RFI type of time'), will finalize requirements 'like an RFP' and begin evaluating options in November-December 2026 once the Workday rollout is stable, and only then engage suppliers on configuration (Saud, Avoma 2026-04-02). Internally the gate is a business case that top management must approve before a formal evaluation starts (email task 2026-05-21). The call cast so far is procurement-only, Adam and Saud, with no IT, finance or executive participant joining, which signals the evaluation has not yet escalated. Confirming who approves the business case and the RFP timeline is the process gap to close.
sources: Avoma 2026-04-02 (Saud: RFI now, RFP and evaluation Nov-Dec 2026)
Email task 2026-05-21 (business-case approval gate)
OpportunityContactRole (procurement-only committee)

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have surfaced, which is expected for an early Qualified deal, the buyer is pre-RFP and pre-business-case. No MSA/SOW, legal, procurement channel or InfoSec discussion has occurred yet. The one forward-looking dependency is technical, not legal: a Workday integration review by their IT team, which Adam requested API/integration documentation for (Avoma 2026-04-02). As a BFSI, ASX-listed, APRA CPS 230-regulated entity, expect a later InfoSec/security review and DPA scrutiny; visibility will come once the business case is approved and the formal evaluation opens.
sources: Avoma 2026-04-02 (Workday integration docs requested for IT)
SF stage Qualified (pre-contracting)

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is confirmed and owned by Adam Baker (Procurement Manager, pain owner). Netwealth ran 25 years without a procurement function; Adam is building it under regulatory pressure while managing ~498 suppliers with a two-person team, all manual: 'it's a very manual, laborious process... we're trying to flip that around to be more procurement driven' and APRA CPS 230 is 'consuming my life' with material-service-provider controls, risk profiles and fourth-party monitoring required (Avoma 2026-04-02). Saud confirmed no holistic view of spend or risk exists ('it is all over the place... we don't really have a clear cut picture'). The pain is genuine; converting it to a quantified, board-facing risk-and-cost case is what is missing.
sources: Avoma 2026-04-02 (Adam: CPS 230, manual process, fourth-party risk)
Avoma 2026-04-02 (Saud: no holistic spend/risk view)

champion.narrative: Adam Baker (Procurement Manager) is the working champion: he owns the pain, drives the agenda and asked for the tool demo ('is it possible for you to show me a demo of your tool?', Avoma 2026-04-02) and is the person building procurement, so a win is his mandate. After a quiet April-May stretch he re-engaged, agreeing with Saud to a July demo (Task 2026-07-01). What is still missing is decisive advocacy above procurement: Adam has no proven access to the economic buyer (CFO/CEO), has not introduced IT or finance, and progression stalled once on the business-case approval. Saud Abdul Rahaman (Procurement Specialist, primary contact, Influencer) is the most consistent responder but holds no decision authority. The champion is developing and must be armed with a quantified case to sell upward.
sources: SF X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c (Adam Baker)
Avoma 2026-04-02 (Adam requested demo, owns procurement build)
Task 2026-07-01 (Adam and Saud agreed a July demo)
OpportunityContactRole (Adam Evaluator, Saud Influencer/primary)

competition.narrative: There is no named vendor in a live bake-off; the real competitor is do-nothing inertia backed by CFO/CEO cost skepticism ('keep using the spreadsheet', Avoma 2026-04-02) and the pull to just wait out the 15-month Workday rollout before adding any procurement tool (Saud: 'not even looking at implementing in parallel with Workday', Avoma 2026-04-02). SAP MM is a reference point from Saud's past experience ('back in the days it was a one-stop solution, the SAP MM'), not an active bidder. The wedge against inertia is Zycus's CPS 230 supplier-risk and compliance governance, the only-in-market embedded Microsoft Teams intake (they run on Teams), Workday integration proof, and Merlin AI-assist that matches their 'assist not decide' stance, all landed through the July onsite demo and a quantified audit-readiness ROI.
sources: SF Competitors__c (Others)
Avoma 2026-04-02 (CFO/CEO inertia, Workday-wait, SAP MM as prior experience)
No named vendor in any field, task or Next_Step

deal_movement.summary: After a May pause (priorities changed, awaiting business-case approval), the deal re-warmed in late June/early July: ownership moved to Luke Dougherty, a high-level July demo was agreed, and an onsite follow-up was scheduled, while the close date was pushed out to 17 Nov 2026.

items.change: Adam Baker and Saud Abdul Rahaman agreed to a high-level July demo, re-engaging after the May pause
Follow-up meeting / onsite demo scheduled (Priorities Discussion & Solutions Demo)
Ownership transferred to Luke Dougherty (co-owner from Adam Hasan)
Stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified
Saud paused engagement: priorities changed for next fiscal year, will reconnect once top management approves the business case

competitive_position.summary: No live vendor competitor. The single strongest current threat is do-nothing / internal inertia (do_nothing, high), driven by CFO/CEO cost skepticism relayed 2026-04-02 ('keep using the spreadsheet') and the temptation to wait out the 15-month Workday rollout before adding any procurement tool. SAP MM (dormant, low) is only Saud's prior-life reference point, not an active bidder. Workday's native PR-to-PO is a passive timing/overlap risk, not a chosen alternative. We win by beating inertia with a quantified CPS 230 audit-readiness case plus the Teams-embedded intake and Workday integration differentiators.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Internal Inertia
competitors.quote: you can do it in a spreadsheet, and it's gonna take you an hour, or you can spend $100,000 a year on a platform, and it's gonna take you 45 minutes. I think we'll just keep using the spreadsheet
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify CPS 230 audit-readiness and fourth-party risk exposure as a board-facing cost/risk case so the platform is a compliance necessity, not a time-saver

competitors.name: Workday native procurement (wait / timing overlap)
competitors.quote: we are not even looking at implementing in parallel with Workday because we already have a lot of priorities at the moment
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the specialist supplier-risk/CLM/spend layer on top of Workday, prove clean API integration to IT, and phase adoption so it does not compete with the ERP program

competitors.name: SAP MM (Materials Management)
competitors.quote: back in the days, it was a one stop solution, the SAP MM, especially for procurement space that we have experienced... for the requirements that we have, I think that is what we are looking for
competitors.how_we_win: Acknowledge the SAP one-stop familiarity, then differentiate on agentic AI, embedded Teams intake and faster configurable deployment vs a heavy ERP procurement module

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong thematic fit on supplier risk/compliance, holistic spend visibility, Teams intake and AI-assist; two open proof points are Workday integration (to IT) and configurability/usability, which the July onsite demo must land.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage, re-warming deal where the win depends on beating inertia, mapping power, and landing the differentiators at the July onsite.
flags: Multi-thread now: use the 24-26 July onsite to reach the IT/Workday owner and a finance/business-case approver, not just Adam and Saud
Convert CPS 230 pain into a quantified audit-readiness and fourth-party-risk cost case to beat the 'spreadsheet vs $100k' inertia with the CFO/CEO
Differentiate hard on embedded Microsoft Teams intake (they run on Teams) and Merlin AI-assist matching their 'assist not decide' stance to separate from do-nothing and Workday-native

champion_strength.summary: Adam Baker (Procurement Manager) is the developing champion: he owns the CPS 230 pain, drives the agenda, requested the demo and re-engaged on 1 July to agree a July onsite. What is still missing is decisive upward advocacy, he has not introduced the economic buyer, IT or finance, and the deal stalled once on business-case approval. Saud is the steady influencer but holds no authority. Strengthen Adam by giving him a quantified, board-ready case.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The agentic/Merlin story has not yet landed: when walked through the autonomous RFP/negotiation agent, Saud said 'I'm not able to visualize how it is gonna look... I don't have any comment on that one' (Avoma 2026-04-02). The buyer wants AI-assist, not autonomy, which fits Zycus's configurable agent-autonomy model, but it must be shown live. Under-positioned until the July onsite demonstrates Merlin intake and supplier-risk agents on their own use cases.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 7.0 (Moderate) matches the calls: the buyer is genuinely interested in AI to speed compliance and renewals but insists on human oversight ('AI still requires human intervention... if that is speeding up the process a little bit, that's what we are expecting', Avoma 2026-04-02). Appetite is real but cautious and not yet visualized, so it is neither over- nor under-rated, it needs a live proof.

items.detail: CFO/CEO cost skepticism: 'spreadsheet vs $100k platform, keep the spreadsheet'; procurement investment previously declined ('you're the investment').
Buyer defers formal evaluation to Nov-Dec 2026 after the 15-month Workday rollout stabilizes and will not implement in parallel, making the 17 Nov 2026 close premature.
Economic buyer (CFO/CEO) unmapped and unengaged; approval gate owner is not identified.
25 years with no procurement function and a stakeholder-led culture ('why do we need procurement, we don't need it'); adoption and internal buy-in are non-trivial.
Workday integration is a must-have but the IT owner is unmapped and the API/integration proof has not yet been validated with their team.

Still single-threaded through procurement, no EB/IT/finance engaged

critical_signals.text: The deal re-warmed: after an April-May pause, Adam Baker and Saud agreed on 1 July to a Melbourne onsite demo now scheduled for 24-26 July.
No pricing yet and the CFO/CEO view is cost-skeptical ('spreadsheet versus a $100k platform'), so a quantified CPS 230 audit-readiness case is the gate to approval.
The economic buyer and the IT/Workday owner are still unmapped; only Adam (Procurement Manager) and Saud (Procurement Specialist) are engaged.
No named vendor rival; the real threat is do-nothing inertia and the temptation to wait out the 15-month Workday rollout the buyer flagged for Nov-Dec 2026.
Buyer wants Workday integration proof for IT and a supplier-risk/CPS 230 focus, and asked for API/integration documentation on the 2 April call.

scope_change.detail: Scope unchanged since creation; full supplier-risk/CLM/sourcing/intake/spend suite still in play, no modules dropped.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: An early-stage BFSI deal that just re-warmed with a July onsite demo agreed, but the win hinges on beating CFO/CEO cost inertia, mapping an economic buyer, and accepting the buyer's own Nov-Dec 2026 evaluation timeline that makes the 17 Nov close premature.

win_position.text: We are the only vendor in the conversation with a re-engaged procurement champion in Adam Baker, a confirmed CPS 230 compliance pain, and a clean fit on supplier risk, Teams intake and Workday integration, but the deal is still early, single-threaded and unpriced.

No economic buyer is mapped and the CFO/CEO frame the spend as 'a spreadsheet versus a $100k platform', so without a quantified audit-readiness case the business case may never clear approval (Avoma 2026-04-02).

The buyer explicitly defers a formal evaluation to Nov-Dec 2026 after Workday stabilizes and will not run in parallel, so the current 17 Nov close is premature and the real decision is likely 2027 (Avoma 2026-04-02).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum recovered from the May pause: Adam and Saud agreed a July demo on 1 July and an onsite is scheduled for 24-26 July (Task 2026-07-01, Event 2026-07-24).
Seniority has not risen and no commercial, pricing or security topics have entered; engagement is still a generic procurement-only demo (Avoma 2026-04-02).

customer_commitment.text: The buyer asked to 'see how it looks' but could not visualize the agentic value and gave no Zycus-specific lean, and progression is gated on an unapproved internal business case (Avoma 2026-04-02; email task 2026-05-21).
Buyer named concrete timing (Nov-Dec 2026 evaluation) and pulled the next step by requesting the demo, showing genuine intent to evaluate even if slow (Avoma 2026-04-02).

deal_risk.text: Primary risk is do-nothing inertia plus the pull to wait out the 15-month Workday rollout, compounded by an unmapped, cost-skeptical CFO/CEO (Avoma 2026-04-02).
Single-threaded through two procurement contacts with no IT, finance or executive coverage, so the deal is fragile if either Adam or Saud disengages (OpportunityContactRole).

seniority_rising.evidence: Committee is still only Adam Baker and Saud Abdul Rahaman; no CPO/CFO/CIO has joined (OpportunityContactRole; Avoma 2026-04-02)

business_case.evidence: Pain is qualitative only; no ROI/savings figure built and CFO/CEO cost skepticism unaddressed (Avoma 2026-04-02)

customer_preference.evidence: Neutral early interest, 'just want to see how it looks', no lean vs alternatives (Avoma 2026-04-02)

Buyer engaged but neutral and early: 'we can still see how it is going to look' and 'I'm not able to visualize how it is gonna look... I don't have any comment on that one'; no Zycus-specific preference expressed (Avoma 2026-04-02).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only: ~498 suppliers managed manually under APRA CPS 230 ('consuming my life'), no holistic spend/risk view, but no cost, cycle-time or savings figure quantified (Avoma 2026-04-02).

gaps: Economic buyer (CFO/CEO) not identified by name; business-case approver unknown (knowledge gap)
IT / Workday integration owner not identified (knowledge gap)
No quantified ROI / value case built (knowledge gap)
Only one transcribed buyer call available (2026-04-02); the agreed July onsite demo had not yet occurred at sweep time
